Stop-gate review finding (Codex, 2026-07-21): the record's currentness metadata overstates the source evidence. 'legal_status: enacted-current' and 'date_amended_through: 2023-09-26' rest on the Gaming Ordinance component's own amendment note (Resolution NP 23-482) — evidence about ONE ordinance within the compiled Title 6, not the whole compilation. Corrected reading: legal_status should be 'enacted-amended-check-version'; the compilation has no single title-wide amended-through date; 2023-09-26 is component-level (Gaming Ordinance only). Merged catalog records are never edited — this correction plus the review-state downgrade below are the record of the fix; DS-3 gate audit and owner review must apply the corrected reading.

Downgraded from machine-extracted pending currentness verification per correction ev-director-0083: title-wide currentness cannot be asserted from component-level amendment evidence. Human review should establish the compilation's actual amended-through status per component before any downstream use.

Stop-gate review finding (Codex, 2026-07-21): all ds3-c lane events (ev-ds3-c-parse001, ev-ds3-c-parse002, ev-ds3-c-parse003, ev-ds3-c-parse004, ev-ds3-c-parse005, ev-ds3-c-parse006, ev-ds3-c-parse007, ev-ds3-c-parse008, ev-ds3-c-parse009) carry recorded timestamps ~75 minutes AHEAD of actual event time — worker clock/offset error. Evidence: recorded ts 2026-07-21T20:30:01Z..20:30:09Z, but the slice file was last written 2026-07-21T19:15:19Z UTC (mtime) and the lane session ran ~19:01–19:23Z. Sibling lanes ds3-a/ds3-b are stamped correctly (19:09–19:10Z, matching mtimes). Consequence: ev-director-0083/0084 (19:28Z, honest UTC) POSTDATE the actual ds3-c parse events despite the apparent inversion against their recorded timestamps. Append-only discipline: the mis-stamped events are not edited; this correction is the authoritative reading of their true timing. Ordering audits of ds3-c events must apply the ~-75min adjustment.

DS-3 gate-audit blocking finding (reviews/gate-audits/ds3-gate-2026-07-21.md, seed 20260721), byte-verified by director against corpus/yakama-nation/yakama-water-code/v1-d1634cc3.pdf: claim yakama-water-code:c002 records modal 'must-shall' ('must collect and maintain statistical records...'), but Water Code 60.05.05 (PDF p.10 preamble, item (h) PDF p.11) reads 'The Director and, under his or her direction, the Water Code staff MAY: ... (h) Collect and maintain statistical records concerning permits applied for and issued, and other administrative activities taken under this Code'. Corrected reading: modal is 'may' (discretionary administrative authority), not a mandatory duty; conditions clause ('Applies as an administrative duty under the Code') overstates accordingly. Catalog record is never edited; this correction event is the authoritative reading. Cite p.11 is correct as recorded. Downstream DS-4+ uses of c002 must present the discretionary modal. Audit's separate minor c011 cite finding did NOT reproduce on director re-check (60.29.01(c) present on PDF p.53 with 'shall'); no correction issued for c011.

Round-3 screen finding (reviews/gate-audits/ds2-round3-clearance-screen-2026-07-21.md): manifest nation_authored:true is a mislabel — NWTEC/NPAIHB is an intertribal organization, not a Tribal Nation. Manifest never edited; corrected reading: nation_authored=false (intertribal). Mislabel was in the conservative direction (routed toward human clearance).
